claim_boundary: The review classifies current contraction candidates; it does not change code or prove post-refactor parity.
do_not_use_when: The task is greenfield structure planning, direct production refactoring, or intended behavior change.

downstream_targets.condition: Ready target actions need module/function ownership design.
A public entrypoint or existing code boundary will be refactored.
Edit order, evidence freshness, or release scope must be governed.

native_checks.summary: Run the tracked architecture-reduction owner tests and known-bad cases.

route_summary: Classify behavior-preserving architecture contraction candidates and their proof/handoff status.

source_requirements.summary: Every candidate must be grounded in an observable contract and model-to-code mapping.
Only equivalence or current public-facade proof can make a contraction ready.
Production edits and public-entrypoint parity remain owned by StructureMesh and DevelopmentProcessFlow.

use_when: Existing model/code ownership suggests the implementation can shrink without changing the observable contract.

workflow.summary: Confirm shrink-oriented scope and the observable behavior that must remain unchanged.
Map existing model ownership, code boundaries, state, side effects, entrypoints, and compatibility surfaces.
Classify merge, collapse, remove, keep-facade, or review candidates with proof status.
Run the native reduction model and reject candidates lacking equivalence, facade, or disposition evidence.
Return ready/scoped/risky/blocked candidates and typed implementation/parity handoffs.
